asi.jest_config: react-native preset, babel-jest, no cache, node env

description: Add transformIgnorePatterns to skip transforming most node_modules (was transforming everything)

asi.hypothesis: transformIgnorePatterns:[] was forcing babel to transform ALL node_modules files; restricting to only needed packages saves ~5s
asi.key_insight: The empty transformIgnorePatterns array was the single biggest perf problem

description: Test-only babel config with single @react-native/babel-preset (removing redundant preset-env + preset-typescript)

asi.hypothesis: Removing redundant babel presets would speed up transforms
asi.rollback_reason: No improvement - within noise of previous 9.46s
asi.next_action_hint: Try maxWorkers tuning, or look at specific slow tests like plugin.test.ts

description: Set maxWorkers=50% to reduce worker overhead (13->6-7 workers). 9.5s->8.4s

asi.hypothesis: Too many workers cause overhead with 36 small test suites
asi.key_insight: Even 2-3 workers perform similarly because bottleneck is single slow suite (InterpolateColor ~8.7s)

description: Split into Jest projects (css vs core) to avoid loading full setup for CSS tests

asi.hypothesis: Separate Jest projects for CSS tests (no setup) and core tests (full setup) would be faster
asi.rollback_reason: Projects overhead made it slower: 9.1s vs 8.4s
asi.next_action_hint: Focus on reducing babel transform time or test execution time itself

description: Disable source maps in babel-jest transform to reduce overhead

asi.hypothesis: Source map generation adds babel overhead that's unnecessary for tests
asi.key_insight: Source maps are small overhead compared to actual babel transforms

description: Use test-only babel config with single @react-native/babel-preset (removes redundant preset-env + preset-typescript)

asi.hypothesis: Removing redundant babel presets from test transform reduces per-file overhead
asi.key_insight: The main babel.config.js had 3 presets doing overlapping work

description: Set maxWorkers=3 (optimal for this test suite size, less IPC overhead than 50%/6-7 workers)

asi.hypothesis: Fewer workers reduce IPC overhead when most tests share similar initialization cost
asi.key_insight: maxWorkers=3 is optimal. 2 is slightly slower (8.1s), 4-7 progressively worse due to IPC overhead

description: Inline react-native preset instead of using preset: 'react-native' (avoid asset transformer config)

asi.hypothesis: Inlining preset config avoids loading unnecessary asset transformers
asi.next_action_hint: Look at reducing babel plugin cost or test-level optimizations

description: Custom test sequencer to run slowest tests first for better parallelization

asi.hypothesis: Running slowest tests first would improve parallel utilization
asi.rollback_reason: Slightly worse (8.13s vs 7.91s) - default sequencer already handles this or the overhead of the custom sequencer negates benefits
asi.next_action_hint: Try reducing babel overhead via plugin exclusion for node_modules or lighter transform

description: Separate transform for node_modules (without reanimated plugin) vs source files

asi.hypothesis: Skip reanimated babel plugin for node_modules transforms
asi.rollback_reason: No improvement (7.96s vs 7.91s) - transformIgnorePatterns already excludes most node_modules so few files go through the heavier transform
asi.next_action_hint: Try reducing the number of babel plugins in @react-native/babel-preset or use ts-jest

description: Tried minimal babel config without @react-native/babel-preset — Flow and JSX syntax not supported

asi.hypothesis: Minimal babel config (preset-env + preset-typescript + preset-react) would be faster
asi.rollback_reason: RN polyfills use Flow syntax (type, mixed) requiring Hermes parser from @react-native/babel-preset. Custom JSX config also didn't work.
asi.next_action_hint: Can't easily replace @react-native/babel-preset - it's needed for Flow support in RN internals. Try other approaches.
